meta.data_integrity_note: Every promise in this file is sourced from either (a) the party's own official manifesto document, or (b) reporting by credible outlets (TNM, The Hindu, Business Standard, The Federal, dtnext). No inferences, no extrapolations, no AI-fabricated content. Categories with insufficient verified data are left intentionally sparse with a source_gap flag.

DMK.name: DMK
DMK.source: Official DMK manifesto PDF — fetched directly from dmk.blob.core.windows.net (505 numbered promises)

welfare_freebies.headline: ₹4,000 cash to every rice card holder; 20 kg free rice; fuel price cuts
promises: ₹4,000 cash stimulus to every ration (rice) card holder from June 2021 — Promise #502
20 kg of free rice to ration card holders — Promise #235
₹100 subsidy on one cooking gas cylinder per ration card holder — Promise #503
Petrol price reduced by ₹5/litre, diesel by ₹4/litre — Promise #504
Aavin milk price reduced from ₹6 to ₹3 per litre — Promise #505
Free bus transport for women on town buses statewide — Promise #501
Free bus transport for senior citizens above 70 years — Promise #497
₹1,500/month financial assistance for senior citizens, widows, abandoned women, disabled persons, Sri Lankan refugees — Promise #330
Kalaignar Canteens in 500 locations at subsidised cost — Promise #331
Night Shelter Houses for people sleeping on pavements — Promise #333
3 LED bulbs at subsidised price to all card holders — Promise #239

education.headline: Ban NEET; free tablets with 4G for students; education loan waiver for under-30s
promises: Legislation to ban NEET exam in Tamil Nadu, seek Presidential assent — Promise #160
Free tablet devices with 4G/5G and 10GB data for higher secondary and college students; wi-fi in all institutions — Promise #163
Education loans for graduates under 30 years waived by government — Promise #159
Separate State Education Policy for Tamil Nadu, opposing Central NEP — Promise #3
Free sanitary napkin vending machines in all govt and govt-aided schools and colleges — Promise #162
Fees for first-generation economically backward students in engineering, medical, agriculture covered — Promise #165
Coaching centres in all districts for IIT, NIT, AIIMS, IIM entrance exams — Promise #167
100% literacy in Tamil Nadu within 3 years — Promise #170
Morning milk for all students in government and govt-aided schools — Promise #169
Tamil language mandatory in all schools including Kendriya Vidyalayas — Promise #493
New universities in Kanchipuram and Erode — Promise #496
Coaching for UPSC, state govt, Railways and bank exams in Chennai, Madurai, Trichy, Coimbatore, Salem, Tirunelveli — Promise #168

employment_economy.headline: 50 lakh jobs in 5 years; 75% private sector reservation for locals; Old Pension Scheme restored
promises: 10 lakh jobs per year, 50 lakh total by 2025-26 via STEP (State Board of Skill Training and Employment Promotion) — Promise #185
75% reservation in private sector jobs for Tamil Nadu locals — Promise #196
₹15,000 crore fund to revive COVID-hit industries at low interest — Promise #192
₹20 lakh low-interest startup loans to 25,000 graduates per year via TIIC — Promise #206
Old Pension Scheme restored, replacing New Pension Scheme — Promise #309
Ad-hoc and contract workers with 10+ years service considered for permanent employment — Promise #153
Welfare boards for 36 unorganised sector employment types revived; pension and medical assistance increased — Promise #151
100% of govt and public sector jobs allocated to Tamil Nadu youth — Promise #495
Right to Skill Training Act for marginalized groups — Promise #186

women.headline: 40% govt job reservation; 12-month maternity leave; ₹24,000 maternity benefit
promises: Women's reservation in govt jobs increased from 30% to 40% — Promise #246
Maternity leave for state govt employees extended from 9 months to 12 months — Promise #243
₹24,000 maternity benefit in 8 monthly instalments under Muthulakshmi Reddy scheme (up from ₹18,000) — Promise #252
Cyber police stations in all districts for cybercrimes against women and children — Promise #254
Separate courts for sexual crime cases for faster justice — Promise #475
Nursing/breastfeeding stations with privacy in all public places — Promise #245
Jobs for unmarried women above 35 years of age — Promise #262
₹50,000 interest-free loan to 1 lakh BPL women for cottage industries — Promise #261
Working women's hostels in all districts — Promise #253
Sexual harassment committees in all factories and industries — Promise #249
25,000 community welfare workers appointed (women as liaison officers in villages) — Promise #191
180 coaching centres in every district to help women graduates gain employment — Promise #180

agriculture_farmers.headline: Farm loan waiver; paddy MSP ₹2,500/quintal; 150-day NREGA at ₹300/day
promises: Farm and jewellery loans of small and medium farmers waived — Promise #33
Paddy support price increased from ₹2,000 to ₹2,500 per quintal — Promise #75
Sugarcane support price increased to ₹4,000 per quintal — Promise #75
NREGA extended from 100 to 150 days; daily wage increased to ₹300 — Promise #42
Agricultural Development Councils to fix MSP for bananas, turmeric, millets, oilseeds, etc. — Promise #35
90% subsidy for drip irrigation for up to 5 acres — Promise #41
₹10,000 subsidy for electric motors to farmers owning under 3 acres — Promise #45
Separate organic farming department and G. Nammazhvar Organic Farming Research Centre — Promises #36, #37
Ban on GMO (genetic modification) technology in Tamil Nadu — Promise #52
Crop insurance compensation even for individual farm damage (not just region-wide calamities) — Promise #57
Annual agricultural budget after consulting farmers' associations — Promise #29
10,000 crore State Water Management Scheme under CM supervision — Promise #89

health.headline: 100% health insurance; 2,000 ambulances; new specialty hospitals in 4 cities
promises: Kalaignar Kaapeetu Thittam revived — 100% healthcare insurance for heart, cancer, kidney conditions — Promise #347
Ambulances under 108 increased to 2,000; 6 ambulances per Panchayat Union — Promise #349
Advanced speciality hospitals in Coimbatore, Tirunelveli, Trichy, Krishnagiri within 3 years — Promise #348
Mobile hospitals for village healthcare access — Promise #349
New medical colleges in districts currently without any — Promise #164
Geriatric wards in all government hospitals — Promise #325
De-addiction centres established; phased approach to alcohol prohibition — Promise #360
Cancer screening for women in all government hospitals — Promise #248
Rotavirus, meningococcal, hepatitis vaccines free for newborns — Promise #353
Immunisation coverage increased to 100% (from current 56%) — Promise #358
Dialysis, nephrology, CCU in all district hospitals — Promise #344

law_order.headline: Police Commission revived; special courts for sexual crimes; cyber police in all districts
promises: Police Commission reconstituted — Promise #384
Separate courts for sexual crimes against women — Promise #475
Cyber police stations in all districts — Promise #254
CCTV in schools, hospitals, bus stands, police stations, temples — Promise #483
₹1 crore compensation to families of police personnel killed on duty — Promise #383
False cases against Sterlite, Hydrocarbon, Methane, Kudankulam protesters withdrawn — Promise #484
Special courts for corruption cases against former ministers — Promise #21
Grievance redressal mechanism for police personnel at district and state level — Promise #385
High-level committee to eradicate Katta Panchayat (kangaroo courts) — Promise #335

environment.headline: Forest cover to 33%; river cleaning at ₹5,000 crore; CNG buses in all towns
promises: Forest cover increased to 33% of Tamil Nadu — Promise #367
CNG buses in all towns and corporation areas — Promise #366
₹5,000 crore scheme to clean and deepen Chennai rivers (Cooum, Adyar, Kusasthalai) — Promise #93
New Groundwater Act replacing the one repealed by ADMK — Promise #95
Ban on illegal borewells in hilly regions — Promise #82
Forest Commission for wildlife conflict resolution and forest land governance — Promise #368
Solar panels replacing traditional power at all govt schools, colleges, temples, offices — Promise #225
₹2,000 crore check dam construction across multiple districts — Promise #90
Chennai Metro Flood Management Committee — Promise #92
Tamil Nadu Conservation Project for river cleaning of Cauvery, Vaigai, Tamirabarani, etc. — Promise #362

governance.headline: Lok Ayukta revived; 100-day grievance resolution; assembly to meet 100 days minimum per year
promises: Lok Ayukta revived for complaints against MLAs and Ministers — Promise #18
Right to Services Act — certificates (community, birth, income, residential) within fixed timeline — Promise #19
All complaints resolved within 100 days; dedicated Ministry for implementation — Promise #377
Legislative Assembly to convene minimum 100 days per year — Promise #376
Live telecast of assembly proceedings — Promise #375
District Collector offices to publicly display status of public complaints — Promise #23
Single window system for investment approvals reintroduced — Promise #199
TNPSC malpractice probe; transparency in recruitment — Promise #485
New districts and upgrading of towns to municipal corporations — Promises #380, #381
DVAC to function independently without government interference — Promise #20

federal_identity.headline: Oppose Hindi imposition; Education to State List; Tamil as official language in Central offices
promises: Fight Hindi imposition; urge all state languages as official languages of India — Promise #4
Remove 'Education' from Concurrent List back to State List — Promise #2
Oppose NEP; formulate separate TN State Education Policy — Promise #3
Tamil as co-official language in central offices and national banks in Tamil Nadu — Promise #5
Oppose privatisation of Railways, PSU banks, LIC — Promises #488, #489, #490
Sethusamudram Shipping Canal Project to be completed — Promise #112
Katchatheevu reclamation efforts to continue — Promise #113
Urge independent international probe into Sri Lanka war crimes against Eelam Tamils — Promise #13
Oppose Central farm laws; repeal ADMK's farm laws aligned with them — Promise #27
Legislative Council (upper house) reconstituted in Tamil Nadu — Promise #374

AIADMK.name: AIADMK
AIADMK.full_name: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam
AIADMK.manifesto_status: FULL — 297 promises (section-wise) imported from admk_manifesto_2026.json; topic cards re-grouped into 10 categories.

welfare_freebies.headline: Special assistance of Rs. 10,000/- to every family to manage the tax burden and price rise.
promises: Special assistance of Rs. 10,000/- to every family to manage the tax burden and price rise.
3 free gas cylinders per year as livelihood assistance: As livelihood assistance, 3 free gas cylinders will be provided annually to the family of Rice Cardholders.
Along with the Pongal gift hamper, a cash amount of Rs. 1,000/- will be given.
To reduce the household workload of homemakers, a free refrigerator (Fridge) will be provided to rice family cardholders.
Along with the free rice currently provided to family cardholders, one kilo of dal and one litre of cooking oil will be provided free of cost.
Nets and equipment will be provided free of cost to inland fishermen through Cooperative Societies.
A monthly pension of Rs. 2,500 will be provided to fishermen above 65 years of age.
A subsidy of up to Rs. 10,000 per person will be provided to women to start one lakh small/micro enterprises in 5 years.
Steps will be taken to continue the Thalikku Thangam and Marriage Assistance Scheme, including free silk dhotis and sarees.
Free sewing machines will be provided to women who complete training at recognized tailoring institutes.
Subsidized loan facilities and a pension scheme for those above 60 years will be implemented for transgender persons.
The loan amount for differently-abled persons will be increased to Rs. 2,00,000/- with a 50% subsidy.

education.headline: Waiver of education loans obtained from banks: To protect the welfare of students and parents, the government will take over and waive…
promises: Waiver of education loans obtained from banks: To protect the welfare of students and parents, the government will take over and waive the education loans of those who borrowed from banks but are unable to pay their EMIs due to unemployment.

Rs. 10 lakh compensation for families of Jallikattu tamers who lose their lives; Rs. 2 lakh for severe injuries. A Government subsidy of Rs. 5 lakh will be provided to organize events. The online registration system will be cancelled and the manual system reinstated.
Loans obtained by differently-abled persons from cooperative banks will be waived.
Loans obtained from cooperative banks by small street vendors operating on pavements in urban areas will be waived.
The medical admission reservation for government school students will be increased from 7.5 percent to 10 percent.
Special steps will be taken to improve mathematical and language skills; 'STEM learning' centers will be established.
The curriculum will be restructured to teach computer science as a sixth subject for classes 6 to 10.
Clauses detrimental to the two-language policy envisioned by Arignar Anna in the New Education Policy are unacceptable.
The currently vacant teacher posts in the School Education Department will be filled.
Climate Change will be included in the students' curriculum.
Free laptops will be provided at the appropriate time to students studying in government and aided colleges.
Kalvi TV (Education TV) will be brought back into use to benefit students.

employment_economy.headline: 100 days employment scheme will be increased to 150 days: The Central Government has announced that the 100 days employment scheme will…
promises: 100 days employment scheme will be increased to 150 days: The Central Government has announced that the 100 days employment scheme will be increased to 125 days. Steps will be taken to implement this rural development scheme to provide up to 150 days of employment.
To encourage the coir industry, electricity tariff concessions will be provided.
Special steps will be taken specifically to boost industrial wealth in the southern districts.
To facilitate industrial investment, industrial estates will be established at specific locations to implement the industrial corridor project in the southern districts.
Chennai will be upgraded as a Medical Tourism hub and foreign exchange will be earned for the medical sector.
To assist entrepreneurs and exporters, a special separate board will be formed to provide skill development training.
To improve the salt production industry, the 'Amma Salt Scheme' will be brought back.
Steps will be taken to establish Pharmaceutical Parks in Chennai and Tirunelveli to increase employment.
A committee including MSME representatives will be formed to analyze problems and facilitate finding solutions.
To encourage industrial development in rural areas, special subsidies will be provided to start agriculture-based and food processing industries.
Additional subsidies will be provided to encourage micro and small industries to generate their own solar power.
Integrated small trade complexes and skill development complexes for artisans will be created in every district.

women.headline: 'Kula Vilakku Scheme' to protect women's welfare: To create economic equality in society, a monthly assistance of Rs. 2,000/- will be…
promises: 'Kula Vilakku Scheme' to protect women's welfare: To create economic equality in society, a monthly assistance of Rs. 2,000/- will be provided to all family cardholders through the Kula Vilakku Scheme. This amount will be deposited directly into the bank account of the female head of the family.

Free bus travel scheme for men, similar to women: A free bus travel scheme will be implemented for men traveling in city buses. Furthermore, the existing free bus travel scheme for women in city buses will continue to be implemented.
Subsidy scheme for purchasing Amma Two-Wheelers: Amma two-wheelers will be provided to 5 lakh working women with a subsidy of Rs. 25,000/-.

Pensions for the elderly and others increased to Rs. 2,000/-: The monthly social security pension provided to the elderly, widows, destitute unmarried women, deserted women, differently-abled persons, and transgender persons under the social pension scheme will be increased from Rs. 1,200/- to Rs. 2,000/-.
Interest-free loans will be provided to women belonging to Muslim, Christian, and other minority communities to start self-employment ventures.
A monthly assistance of Rs. 2,000 will be provided to unemployed graduate youth and young women; Rs. 1,000 per month will be provided to those who have studied up to +2.
Every year for the Deepavali festival, high-quality free sarees will be provided to all women holding family cards, and high-quality dhotis will be provided to men.
To ensure the safety of women at all times, the Kavalan app functioning in Chennai will be expanded to other cities.
The Amma Patrol vehicles introduced in Chennai for the protection of women and children will be expanded to other cities.
A large number of working women's hostels will be built in urban areas to facilitate safe accommodation.

agriculture_farmers.headline: The relief assistance currently provided to fishermen during the fishing ban period will be increased from Rs. 8,000 to Rs. 12,000.
promises: The relief assistance currently provided to fishermen during the fishing ban period will be increased from Rs. 8,000 to Rs. 12,000.
Free electricity currently provided to handloom weavers will be increased to 450 units, and for powerloom weavers, to 1400 units.
Considering the cost of paddy production, steps will be taken to fix the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for paddy at Rs. 3,500/- per quintal.
Along with encouraging the formation of Farmer Producer Groups/Companies, necessary financial and other assistance will be provided to strengthen existing companies.
To purchase inputs like fertilizers and seeds, a production subsidy of up to Rs. 15,000/- per hectare per year will be provided to small and marginal farmers.
Steps will be taken to waive the agricultural crop loans obtained by farmers through Primary Agricultural Cooperative Credit Societies.
As per the recommendations of Dr. M.S. Swaminathan, a Minimum Support Price (MSP) will be fixed for all food grain agricultural produce.
Steps will be taken so that farmers only pay 2% of the premium, and the government will bear the remaining insurance amount.
Special subsidies will be provided to establish agricultural warehouses as needed by Farmer Producer Groups.
To protect agricultural produce without damage, large-scale storage warehouses, drying machines, and cold storage facilities will be established in required locations.
Additional funds will be allocated to the Agricultural University for research activities to pave the way for agricultural development.
A census of landless agricultural laborers will be conducted, and special steps taken to ensure they receive housing, education, and employment.

health.headline: The approximately 2000 Amma Mini Clinics will be reopened with modern medical facilities.
promises: The approximately 2000 Amma Mini Clinics will be reopened with modern medical facilities.
The government will directly pay the entire amount without any cost limit for major treatments in private hospitals for those in the insurance scheme.
Vacant posts for doctors, dentists, Assistant Professors, and Associate Professors will be filled.
The services of nurses who have been working for more than 10 years will be made permanent.
Under the Amma Master Health Checkup scheme, PET scanning facilities will be established to detect cancer.
Medical camps will be conducted in rural areas to identify people affected by diabetes and blood pressure.
Free IVF treatment will be provided under the Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme.
Linear Accelerator facilities and cancer treatment centers will be established in all district government hospitals.
Infrastructure facilities of Primary Health Centers and all government hospitals will be upgraded.

law_order.headline: Police personnel working in the police department will be provided one day of leave per week.
promises: Police personnel working in the police department will be provided one day of leave per week.
Police welfare hospitals will be established in major cities like Madurai, Coimbatore, and Trichy.
To completely eradicate drugs, an Anti-Drug Task Force will be formed under the direct control of the Chief Minister.
Medical Help Desks will be established in police quarters with a high number of residences.
The government will bear the entire cost if police personnel and their family members undergo life-saving surgeries.
To maintain good relations, a Police-Public Association will be formed under a DSP or Assistant Commissioner.
The uniform allowance currently provided to police personnel will be increased to Rs. 6,000/-.
Steps will be taken to increase the currently low number of working days provided to Home Guards.
The AIADMK will urge the swift establishment of a Supreme Court bench in Chennai.

environment.headline: Registered members of the Auto Drivers Association who do not own an auto will be provided a subsidy of Rs. 75,000/- to purchase a new auto.
promises: Registered members of the Auto Drivers Association who do not own an auto will be provided a subsidy of Rs. 75,000/- to purchase a new auto.
The 'Nadanthai Vaazhi Cauveri Scheme' will be implemented swiftly to prevent sewage from mixing in the river.
Proper studies will be conducted and steps taken to prevent sewage from mixing in rivers like Vaigai and Thamirabarani.
Technology using robotic machines to remove sewage waste will be introduced.
Importance will be given to water recycling and sewage treatment in all municipal corporations.
In highly populated village panchayats, steps will be taken to recycle garbage waste and properly treat sewage water.
To prevent human-elephant conflict, the forest department will be provided with adequate new Jeep vehicles and equipment.

governance.headline: 'Amma Illam Scheme' providing houses for homeless families: Through the 'Amma Illam Scheme,' the government itself will purchase land…
promises: 'Amma Illam Scheme' providing houses for homeless families: Through the 'Amma Illam Scheme,' the government itself will purchase land and build free concrete houses for those in rural areas who do not own a house plot or a house. Similarly, for the homeless poor in urban areas, multi-story apartments will be built and provided free of cost through the 'Amma Illam Scheme.' Under this scheme, Scheduled Caste people living in a joint family will be given priority.
To ensure that the public quickly receives essential documents within specified days, infrastructure like public service centers (E-Seva) will be strengthened.
The website will be upgraded so that the public can directly know all decisions and track actions taken on their petitions.
Severe action will be taken against officials and ministers who have engaged in deep-rooted corruption across departments over the past 5 years.
All pattas previously issued to tribal people living in hilly areas will be computerized, and proper pattas will be issued.
To find a solution to temple Inam land ownership disputes, a committee will be formed and an appropriate solution found.
Arrangements will be made to issue pattas to people residing for a long time on unobjectionable government lands provided they pay the amount fixed by the government.
Whenever the price of copra coconut drops, steps will be taken by the government itself to procure copra.
World-class government animal shelters will be established in every district to care for destitute animals.
Traditional folk arts will be documented and encouraged by the government.
We will urge the Central Government to swiftly conduct a caste-based census.
A special committee will be formed to implement the Rehabilitation and Resettlement Policy for NLC land providers.

federal_identity.headline: Standing firm, we will demand that Tamil Nadu receives its financial share without any reduction.
promises: Standing firm, we will demand that Tamil Nadu receives its financial share without any reduction.
We will continuously insist that Tamil Nadu's current proportional representation is not reduced during the delimitation of constituencies.
We will urge the Central Government to declare state languages, including Tamil, as official languages of the Central Government.
Steps will be taken to bring Tamil as the language of proceedings in the Madras High Court.
We will insist that all examinations, including Central Government competitive exams, be conducted in Tamil.
Financial assistance will be provided to establish Tamil Chairs in 25 international universities.
Firm steps will be taken to retrieve Katchatheevu, which was the dream of Puratchi Thalaivi Amma.
We will urge the Central Government to grant "Dual Citizenship" to Sri Lankan Tamil refugees living in India.

TVK.name: TVK
TVK.manifesto_status: PARTIAL — Full manifesto not yet released (as of Mar 14, 2026). Only women-specific promises officially unveiled on Mar 7, 2026. Other positions sourced from verified speeches and official party statements.

health.headline: Upgrade govt hospitals so people can use them without fear

welfare_freebies.source_confidence: HIGH — TNM article lists specific promises
welfare_freebies.headline: Free driving licences for young women; free tablets; door-delivery of ration
promises: Free two-wheeler driving licence for women aged 18-23, no test fee
Free tablet devices for students of Classes 8 and 9
Door delivery of ration items through Public Distribution System
Widows pension increased to ₹3,000
₹6,000 annual support for fishermen and farmers

education.headline: NEET/JEE coaching in every district; online skill university; tablets for students
promises: Free tablet for students of Classes 8 and 9
Coaching centres for NEET, JEE and CLAT in every district
Online skill development university to be set up
Siddha medicine university to be established in Palani

employment_economy.headline: 50 lakh new jobs in 5 years
promises: 50 lakh new jobs created in 5 years
12 lakh acres of Panchami lands retrieved and distributed to Scheduled Castes

women.headline: Free driving licences for women aged 18-23
promises: Free two-wheeler driving licence for women aged 18-23

agriculture_farmers.headline: ₹6,000 annual support; separate agricultural budget; 5-year sand mining ban
promises: ₹6,000 annual support for farmers
Separate budget for agriculture
River sand mining banned for 5 years to improve groundwater; river sand import allowed during this period

health.headline: Multispecialty hospital in every district with free treatment
promises: Every district to get a government multispecialty hospital with facilities equivalent to private hospitals
Free treatment in these hospitals

law_order.headline: Law against forced religious conversions
promises: Law to be brought against forced religious conversions
Hindu temple administration handed to a Board with religious leaders

environment.headline: 5-year river sand mining ban to restore groundwater and river flow
promises: River sand mining banned for 5 years; river sand import permitted during this period

governance.headline: Legislative council; Greater Chennai trifurcated; Circuit Bench in Coimbatore
promises: Legislative council set up for informed debates by subject-matter experts
Greater Chennai Corporation to be trifurcated
